mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a mean forward return below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product confirm sustained economic growth and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates correspond to negative 60-day forward returns in over 50% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates signals severe labor market contraction and reduced household income, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and industrial production confirm synchronized demand and supply expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and INDPRO yield negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations during expansion phases.
